entry.title: Re: It is not possible to debug programs in SAP GUI for HTML
entry.content:
Hi Ryan,

Yes one should be able to enable external breakpoints and use this, but I cannot.

Hi Junwu,

They have moved into the cloud and therefore a normal GUI is not available.

Hi Sandra,

Thank you for your input, which I always value so much. I have tried those 3 links already, and others and unfortunately it hasn't solved the issue. I will log it with SAP.

Thank you all, and I wish you all a nice evening further.

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: T@sk 2 😎

entry.title: SAP Developer News, August 15th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/08/15/sap-developer-news-august-15th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd

  • Devtoberfest group: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest sessions: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events

Generative AI based Integration Flows and expose events via AIF

  • Generative AI based Integration Flow Generation: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-integration-suite-generative-ai-based-integration-flow-generation/ba-p/13761166                                                                                                     
  • SAP AIF can now expose events to Advanced Event Mesh to power your EDA: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/a-natural-choice-sap-aif-can-now-expose-events-to-advanced-event-mesh-to/ba-p/13773758
  • SAP Note 3474406 - AIFAEM integration: https://me.sap.com/notes/3474406/E

CodeJam Release: CAP AI CodeJam - Expose capabilities of SAP AI Core with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

  • CAP AI CodeJam GitHub repository: https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-cap-llm
  • Overview SAP CodeJams: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407
  • RAG with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine, GenAI Hub & CAP Live Stream: https://www.youtube.com/live/EkEbUYTfa6Q?si=LLS_EHRCW2AimvxO

ABAP Developer Challenge Week 2

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/task-2-cds-view-entity-associations-august-developer-challenge/m-p/13794132

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Devtoberfest by SAP TechEd

0:41 Generative AI based Integration Flows and expose events via AIF

1:29 CAP AI CodeJam - Expose capabilities of SAP AI Core with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

2:04 ABAP Developer Challenge Week 2

Transcript

[Nico] Hi everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. Devtoberfest is just around the corner, starting on September 23rd, and the Devtoberfest group in the SAP community is already live. Go there to read about the official contest and its rules, and also about the grand prize. Devtoberfest sessions are already being scheduled in the SAP Community as well, so go there to check them out, and hope to see you there. Bye!

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. In case you missed it, it is now possible to generate Integration Flows in Cloud Integration. You will just need to describe your integration scenario, and based on that description, you will create an Integration Flow. It is a new feature in Cloud Integration, which you will need to enable in the settings of your tenant. Check out Deepak's blog post for more information.

Also, you can now expose events to SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Mesh by using the Application Interface Framework. What's cool about this is that you can even enable your on-premises ERP, for example, an ECC or an SAP S/4HANA. It is easy to create custom events by leveraging AIF. Check out Kosta's blog post for more details.

[Kevin] I'm really excited to announce a brand new CodeJam evolving around the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model and the CAP LLM Plugin. This CodeJam will teach you how to build services on top of SAP AI Core and the SAP Generative AI Hub capabilities. So if you want to learn more about AI and how to build services on top of AI, follow the links in the description below to check out the CodeJam content on GitHub and check out all the additional information on our community platform. So see you in the next CodeJam.

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here. I hope you're all having a great summer and I also hope that everyone is having a little fun with this month's Developer Challenge. Sheena and Shilpa kicked off your ABAP Developer Challenge last week with Task 1 and there was a huge response. Of course there was. It's ABAP and who doesn't love ABAP, right? I even found some time to complete Task 1 myself and I had a lot of fun doing it. This week's task was just released yesterday and already we have over 50 submissions out there. So I guess I better catch up and get going with Task 2 myself which is on the topic of CDS associations. So that's it for now. If you have a couple minutes for fun learning and a little challenge, check out the Developer Advocates ABAP Developer Challenge which is running now till the end of August. I'll put a link in the show notes. Bye for now!

entry.title: Cloning a Command in SAP Automation Pilot
entry.content:
I am able to successfully trigger the HttpRequest command in AP and it retrieves a 200 response from the GET request.

Then I cloned the HttpRequest and gave it a different name, putting it into one of my catalogs.

When I trigger the cloned command, using the same exact values/parameters that I did with HttpRequest, for some reason, there is nothing in the Output Values.

I'm stumped on this one.  Has anyone run into this before and if so, what do I need to do to correct it?

Thanks in advance!

Ron

entry.title: Re: Are chatbots still supported or recommended for SAP B...
entry.content:
Hi @GeorgeAbraham,

In the absence of an SAP-provided chatbot, I can think of the following options to evaluate:

  1. Inject a custom chatbot using a shell plugin: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-advanced-edition/sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition/add-shell-plugin
    Even if this proved to be technically possible, you would still need to develop an effective approach to continuously train the chatbot based on the customer's SuccessFactors Work Zone content.

  2. SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone Guided Experiences

entry.title: SAP Social Sabbatical – Reflections & Gratitude
entry.content:
Just before SAP Sapphire in Orlando this year, I returned from my SAP Social Sabbatical program in Kenya. I am sharing with you my take on this incredible program because I realize that many are not familiar with it, yet the program presents an amazing opportunity for all SAP catalysts and executives to drive positive change - both within our communities and for our personal and professional growth.

SAP Social Sabbatical is an award-winning portfolio of global volunteering consultancy programs that empower SAP employees to share their knowledge, skills, and expertise with non-profits and social enterprises all around the world. In its purest form, the social enterprise is a real, income-generating business, but one that has a clear social mission. It is a pioneering and burgeoning sector, numbering in the millions worldwide.
SAP is not a social enterprise, but we are inspired by them and our Social Sabbatical program is an opportunity to engage with these inspiring ventures and their visionary founders. It is also an opportunity to experience SAP's purpose - to help the world run better and improve people’s lives - on a very tacit and personal level.

To date, the program has positively impacted 7M+ lives, helped create 460+ jobs, and supported 531 organizations worldwide through 1,601 SAP employees, of which 47% have been our women colleagues. Within it, there are various global, regional, and local programs tailored for SAP executives and the talent in the Catalyst Development Program, so everyone can find an opportunity to volunteer for something suited to their skillset

Personally, I had stepped away from my day-to-day responsibilities for two weeks to participate in the Kenya program, but I came back with years of energy and positivity to give back to our business, our team, SAP and the communities we serve. That amount of motivation, drive and purpose, is the tangible outcome of our Social Sabbatical program I would like all of you to experience.

I met very young people who already know their potential, and who are incredibly capable. They are executing very strategic world-class programs and leveraging technology and AI as much as some of the most capable in our teams in SAP today. But, while globalization and technology have made education more accessible to all over the last couple of decades, they couldn’t offer access to equal local and global opportunities at the same pace. SAP stepped up to help through the best we have to offer – access to markets, our technology and our incredible and skilled talent.

I supported a social enterprise called Taka Taka Ni Mali, founded by one of Kenya’s most successful women entrepreneurs who was named the Woman Entrepreneur of the Year in Kenya in 2018. The name of her enterprise in Swahili means ‘Waste is Wealth’ as the business focuses on managing and recycling waste, with a vision to turn Kenya into an environmentally sustainable nation. The business relies on donors to fund their enterprise, but Mary, the founder, is intent on making Taka Taka Ni Mali a financially independent enterprise with sustainable long-term success.

We were tasked with helping the business explore monetization opportunities to self-fund its social mission. Two weeks later, two of my SAP colleagues and I, representing three different SAP Board areas, redesigned Taka Taka Ni Mali’s entire business model, identifying five key monetization opportunities, and building the financial model and business plan for two of them. The plan was adopted, and the execution started while we were still in Nairobi!
These two weeks had a massive impact on my personal growth too.

Being taken out of my comfort zone - from the daily hum and drum of the business world - and being thrown into a problem that affected the lives of the people in front of me, brought a new level of clarity, efficiency and personal motivation to resolve that problem! The importance of finding a personal connection between the problem and my motivation to solve it, is one of the key lessons from this experience for me.

I was also successfully reminded about the power of conviction – seeing the determination and the absence of fear to go after one’s conviction even in such challenging environments, reignited my personal conviction in the journey we have charted for our Digital motion. I come back stoked, knowing that a few determined individuals can indeed move mountains. We, on the other hand, are a +1,000 men and woman-strong team in the Customer Success Digital motion – our goals need to remain incredibly ambitious - because we CAN attain them.

SAP is setting an incredibly high bar in the industry with this program and I encourage you all to check if you are eligible to participate at least once in your career. Programs like these help us align our actions with the values we stand for and the culture we strive to uphold - where empathy and kindness can be, and are, executed in harmony with our grit and high performance.

I’ve always been proud to be an SAPer, but I’ve never been as proud as I am now!

entry.title: select from cust. table using SAP CPQ 2.0 - How to Use " Table Tags " to get data into Form Directly
entry.content:
I want to know how to use Table Tag inside Document Generation

for example if i want to use the following Tag :

Spoiler
<* TABLE ( SELECT EN FROM DocCont_AddtionalDetail WHERE OutputType = 'Quote' AND ContentSections = 'Footer' ) *> 


to get Data From a Custom Table Inside the Word Document, Unfortunately it used to work but now on the Current Version 2408, it doesn't work anymore , Do you have any suggestions ?!

entry.title: Re: Pregunta de Garantias
entry.content: Hola buenas tardes, incluso si son campos de usuario de la OINV o alguna otra nativa de SAP??

entry.title: SAP CPQ 2.0 - How to Use " Table Tags " to get data into Form Directly "Generated Form"
entry.content:
I want to know how to use Table Tag inside Document Generation

for example if i want to use the following Tag :

Spoiler
<* TABLE ( SELECT EN FROM DocCont_AddtionalDetail WHERE OutputType = 'Quote' AND ContentSections = 'Footer' ) *> 


to get Data From a Custom Table Inside the Word Document, Unfortunately it used to work but now on the Current Version 2408, it doesn't work anymore , Do you have any suggestions ?!

entry.title: Re: Extending Fiori Elements, make a field read-only
entry.content:
Looking at the original app source, I realized that the field I want to override is part of an SAP-written extension to the List Report FE, so I cannot override the methods in this extension.

In that extension controller they have a method that I need to override:

Does anyone know how to extend and override an extension controller of an FE app?

There must be a way using extensionAPI object.

Thanks.

entry.title: Erro FIEBPP-112 intergração de retorno bacario FF.5
entry.content:
Olá, pesso apoio da comunidade para que eu possa encontar uma solução do erro FIEBPP-112
Esta mensagem esta configurada como alto explicativa, porem não é possivel identificar o motivo do erro.

Ao executar a integração de um arquivo de retorno bancario atravez da FF.5, ao processar um documento que consta como codigo de retono definido como "DV - Pagamento não confirmado" o sistema deve efetuar o estorno de forma automatica, porem estou recebendo uma mensagem de erro.

Verificando durante o debug notei que existe um comentario ao lado que indica que foi implementada um Sap Note, mas pesquisando por esse numero não localizei qualquer documentação ou menção a estas notas. 

Verifiquei na SNOTE em meu sistema e não localizei estas notas instaladas.

Verifiquei que na tabela BSEGC não possue dados.

Alguem poderia dar uma direção de como identificar uma forma de corrigir este caso.
#ECC

entry.title: SAP PERSONAS
entry.content:
SAP Screen Personas 3.0

 Install SAP Screen Personas 3.0

Activate Service and Maintain Parameter in T-Code: SICF

1. /default_host/sap/bc/personas

2. /default_host/sap/bc/personas3

3. /default_host/sap/bc/gui/sap/its/webgui

Change SAP GUI them to Blue Crystal

In bottom right corner of SAP screen ‘P’ icon will come, confirming installation and activation of Personas

Assign correct access to required users as per below

 

  1. Administrator: /PERSONAS/ADMIN_ROLE - Provides full access to all available features of SAP Screen Personas, the design time, runtime, and the administrative environment.
  2. Flavor Builder: /PERSONAS/EDITOR_ROLE - Provides full access to the design time editors, the client, and themes, but not the administrative environment.
  3. Flavor Consumer: /PERSONAS/CONSUMER_ROLE - Provides authorization rights for read-only access to SAP Screen Personas flavors and the navigation between available flavors in the Flavor Manager

Note: Make sure to generate above mentioned SAP standard role or copy it to Z/Y role before assigning it to users.

Launch SAP Person on SAP GUI for HTML

Link: http(s)://host.domain:port/sap/bc/personas

Blue line with ‘P’ icon would appear on screen

Hover over ‘P’ icon and you will see option to see create Flavor or/and any existing flavor for that screen.

Use Case; VA02 (Change Sales Order)

 By default, if we open any order in VA02, it will show below tabs:

Using Personas we will hide ‘Item Overview’ & ‘Ordering Party’

  • Open VA02 and open any sales order and navigate to above screen
  • Click on ‘+’ to create new flavor

Select ‘Item Overview’ and right click and select ‘HIDE’ from options, likewise, do the same for ‘Ordering Party’. Perform all required operations in any field or box required within VA02.

I have changed the name of field ‘Net Value’ to ‘Total Value’ and hide ‘Item Overview’ & ‘Ordering Party’ tab.

Exit and save from top right and your flavor would appear, you can mark that as default to see the screen with changes or switch to original.

Right click on your flavor and you can assign it to user for testing. (Alternatively you can do flavor user assignment directly through T-Code : /N/PERSONAS/ADMIN)

 

T-Code : /N/PERSONAS/ADMIN

Create test user with access to VA02(you can assign Fiori App as well) + /PERSONAS/CONSUMER_ROLE or/and any basic role to access Fiori or system and assign this user to ‘DEMO_PERSONAS’ created above.

Test User: TEST_PERSONA

Login using ‘TEST_PERSONA’ and open VA02, on top right ‘P’ icon would appear, click on that, it will give option to select flavor.(There is also way to make flavor as default and prevent user from selecting, I’ve discussed about it later)

You can select “Demo_Personas” and see VA02 without ‘Item Overview’ & ‘Ordering Party’ tab.

Next you can select “Original Screen” and see VA02 with ‘Item Overview’ & ‘Ordering Party’ tab.

Same will work on Fiori Tiles.

How to make only 1 flavor available for user.

Create new role as copy of /PERSONAS/CONSUMER_ROLE, deactivate option “Selecting flavors” in object P_RUNTIME and assign this new role to user and remove /PERSONAS/CONSUMER_ROLE from user.

entry.title: Re: Cheat sheet for URI patterns for calling OData actions and functions
entry.content:
@MatthiasH 

Can you try crating a list report with the function as a button on the table toolbar? If that is possible, you can check the network request to see how the URL is constructed.

entry.title: Does SAP GUI 32bit work well with WebView2 64bit?
entry.content:
Will the WebView2 that is integrated with Windows 11 64-bit work with SAP GUI for Windows 32-bit?  Can the 32-bit SAP GUI for Windows be run with the 64-bit Webview2? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Does SAP GUI 32bit work well with WebView2 64bit?
entry.content:
Hello,

All known differences between using SAP GUI 32bit or SAP GUI 64bit are documented in 3218166 - SAP GUI for Windows: Functional differences of the 64bit version compared to the 32bit version.

Therefore, as per the indicated note, there's no limitation for using SAP GUI 32bit with WebView2 64bit.

Thank you!

entry.title: start-of-selection and sy-ucomm
entry.content:
sy-ucomm

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  IF sy-ucomm = 'BTN1' AND lv_executed IS INITIAL.
    "lv_executed = abap_true.

    CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
      EXPORTING
        nr_range_nr             = '01'
        object                  = 'ZNRO_TEST2'
      IMPORTING
        number                  = lv_number
      EXCEPTIONS
        interval_not_found      = 1
        number_range_not_intern = 2
        object_not_found        = 3
        OTHERS                  = 4.

    gs_log-id = lv_number.
    gs_log-ad = gv_ad.
    gs_log-soyad = gv_soyad.
    gs_log-yas = gv_yas.

    INSERT zbk_egt_00034 FROM gs_log.


  ENDIF.

In this statement, If statement triggers twice. Because of that program print 2 rows. What could be the reason ?

entry.title: Re: What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – June & July 2024
entry.content:
Very Helpful information.

Thanks,

Rashmi

entry.title: Re: Publishing contents
entry.content: Are you trying to Export or Publish? Export means you are saving a project as a SCORM, exe, HTML or doc/pdf/ppt elsewhere. Publishing is when you want Learners to access the content in SAP Enable Now Manager.

entry.title: Re: Anniversary Tile for Managers
entry.content: Thank you so much! I appreciate your response!

entry.title: Re: 🇵🇹 Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA (Porto, PT)
entry.content: If you are interested in registering, please submit your name, email and telephone number to sapinsidetrackporto@gmail.com

entry.title: Re: S/4 Hana : Consume CDS view in Query Browser
entry.content:
I tried creating a view entity but it does not appear in query browser.

Does this only work with CDS views and not view entities? Do you need the abapcatalog.sqlviewname annotation for this to work?

What about when SAP deprecates CDS views in 7.57 .. ?

entry.title: Re: SAC Content Transport (Migration) Using CTMS
entry.content: How to integrate CTMS (activated in CALM subaccount) and SAC?

entry.title: SAP Collaborative ERP update for 2408. Share as cards, tab, or chat.
entry.content:
Our collaboration features are gaining popularity among SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers. Now, we have added a new capability to share content from an SAP Fiori elements app as an adaptive card through Microsoft Teams. This is available in the 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This update improves collaboration with your co-workers by providing another option to interact with a single source of data in your SAP S/4HANA system. You can now share application data with your colleagues as a card, tab, or chat.

Share as Card makes SAP S/4HANA data more portable

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers, a new "Share as Card" option is now available in the Microsoft Teams share menu.

This feature allows you to work directly within Microsoft Teams and execute actions right from your Teams environment.

The adaptive card in Microsoft Teams lets you perform simple tasks that will update your SAP S/4HANA Cloud data seamlessly.

Chat directly from the SAP app

In addition, when using SAP Fiori elements applications, you can now start a chat, video call, or voice call with your co-worker directly from within the app. This feature facilitates real-time communication for discussing issues or seeking clarification.

Actions come to you as To Do notifications

For early adopters a new Notifications feature is enabled in Microsoft Teams.

Receive notifications for tasks that are in your My Home page in SAP S/4HANA Cloud directly in your Microsoft Teams Activity Feed, enabling you to quickly begin working on tasks right from Teams.

Lots of great collaboration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

What’s new in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408?

  • Share as Card generally available
  • Contacts generally available
  • To Do’s in early adopter care

What’s next

We are continuing to enhance the integrations between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and Microsoft Teams. As was announced at SAP Sapphire, we will include new integrations between SAP Joule and Microsoft CoPilot. This will provide even more opportunities for collaboration between human experts when AI doesn’t have all the answers.

We’re looking forward to your feedback!

entry.title: Re: Popover will close unexpectedly, when display a target in the component
entry.content:
In case somebody else come accross this problem... Here is how to solve it:

// setup router in the embedded component
const router = this.getRouter();
const targetHandler = router.getTargetHandler();

targetHandler.setCloseDialogs(false);

router.initialize();

entry.title: HI, I am having 15 years in experience in Accounts and Finance, I have completed SAP FICO training.
entry.content:
HI, I am having 15 years in experience in Accounts and Finance, I have completed SAP FICO training.

I have no practical experience by I have good theoretical knowledge in SAP FICO, I need practical knowledge in implementation projects.

Please support to do.

entry.title: SAP’s Enterprise Automation helped avoid risk exposure over 1.9 Billion USD in un-invoiced spending
entry.content:
Surge in AI-powered automation to gain a competitive edge

In today's digital age, companies are adapting to new business strategies that rely heavily on artificial intelligence to streamline their operations. While some businesses have invested in automation initiatives, many have a disjointed approach that fails to deliver the desired results. To overcome this issue, companies are now turning to Enterprise Automation - a complete solution that integrates AI-powered tools to model and automate business and IT processes. By adopting this approach, businesses can unify their heterogeneous landscape, monitor their processes, make continuous improvements, and achieve complete automation.

 

Background

Customer is a large American multinational Information Technology company that develops personal computers, printers and related supplies, and 3D printing-related solutions. The customer’s vision is to become an Intelligent Enterprise powered by SAP BTP. 

Motivation: Optimize Business Processes and Lower Operational Costs

SAP Build Process Automation helps to rapidly address process inefficiencies by building AI-powered automation and extending applications using low-code solutions and pre-built content. It enables citizen developers to leverage process expertise without coding to build automation using drag-and-drop simplicity by combining workflow management, task automation, and AI in a single solution.

As part of their transformation initiatives customer consolidated siloed operations to improve their business processes through automation and artificial intelligence. As a first step, SAP Signavio Process Insights was used to obtain fast insights into their business processes. The tool identified bottlenecks and optimization opportunities using accelerators provided by SAP Build Products.

 

Key Business Challenges

  • There are over 65K open Purchase Orders without any changes in SAP Ariba, which had to be cleaned up.
  • Ensure process compliance, eliminate Risk exposure of Approved Open POs
  • Compliance and avoidance of Risk exposure of over 1.9 billion un-invoiced spend
  • Establish an Enterprise Platform for automating and improving business processes by citizen developers

 

MaxAttention Engagement

The SAP MaxAttention team was onboarded to analyze SAP Signavio Process Insights within their business processes and to identify any bottlenecks. Based on an objective assessment of their process performance indicators and process blockers, the team identified many Purchase Orders that had not been changed for over 18 months.

The SAP MaxAttention team was further engaged to assess and resolve existing integration issues by replicating purchase orders between SAP systems and developing automation to close open purchase orders based on business-defined criteria. The expectation from the customer was to quickly realize this automation to demonstrate the key capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation and establish it as a foundation for their citizen developer-centric automation. 

 

Solution Architecture

Below is the architecture of “PO AutoClose” automation.

Process Flow

The following image illustrates the overall end-to-end flow of the productive solution Landscape:  

 

Delivery Scope

Assessment of the functional scope

There were over 100K Purchase Orders in SAP Ariba out of which over 65K Purchase Orders were not changed in over 18 months.

Development of Automation project

Designed and developed hybrid automation involving SAP Ariba and SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP Build Process Automation. The automation can support attended as well as unattended execution. SAP MaxAttention team realized the PoC effectively in less than 3 weeks after the finalization of the functional scope.

Deployment to Production Support

The SAP MaxAttention team proposed an engagement model to safeguard the investment from the customer to deploy the PoC to production:

  1. Configured customer’s Ariba Procurement realm and Cloud Integration Gateway (CIG) portal to leverage the standard integration offered by SAP Ariba for updating the PO closure based on the implemented automation
  2. Supported customer in developing the strategy to identify the list of POs eligible for closure

Key Achievements

  • Automated solution scalable to be used by the customer for all PO Closure going forward
  • Advantage of having a quick low code, no code automation solution catering to digital transformation requirements but also helping to keep the S/4 Core clean. 
  • Ensures compliance, and eliminates Risk exposure of Approved Open POs
  • Scalable to all payable processes enterprise-wide

Key Customer Outcomes

Contact persons for follow-up: Kiran Kola, Rohit Dwivedi, Victor Chan, Kalaimani Tamilarasu

SAP Build Product Management: Steven Jacobson, Andre Borchert

entry.title: Impact of a high change rate transactional volume on downtime-optimized Conversion to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
With the fast-approaching end of maintenance coming on December 31st 2027 for SAP ERP 6.0 most customers are in the midst of planning their transitioning to SAP S/4HANA. If the brownfield system conversion is chosen, the business downtime window restriction will be the key deciding factor on potentially whether any downtime-minimization approach will be required.

Enter downtime-optimized Conversion (doC) for the Software Update Manager (SUM) to allow for a single step minimized approach for brownfield conversions that includes migrating to SAP HANA Database while converting to SAP S/4HANA. Starting end of calendar year 2023, DMOVE2S4 is supporting doC for migrating to hyperscaler as generally available under certain restrictions.

While using the doC approach, three long-running Technical Downtime activities are moved partially to the Uptime with only delta remaining for your maintenance window. This delta is one of the main Technical Downtime estimation challenges when working on doC projects as converting non-Production systems won’t have any delta to process. Due to the nature of how the delta is processed, even simulated data on lower landscapes might not be completely representative of what you might found during actual Production Go-Live.

Having a high change rate transactional volume possess an additional challenge when estimating the technical downtime as the business generated data will impact directly and indirectly some of the technical downtime steps (during the SUM Execution Roadmap phases). Following up the impact on the following activities will be explored:

  • Uptime migration of tables: downtime-optimized migration of the manually selected application tables during uptime
  • Downtime migration of tables: final migration of the application data not selected for uptime migration
  • Downtime FI and ML conversions: finance and Material Ledger related delta conversion activities
  • Other data conversions: remaining table structure conversions and miscellaneous items to complete the loop

 

Uptime migration of tables or downtime-optimized DMO (doDMO)

For the uptime migration of tables doC leverages the proven solution in downtime-optimized DMO (doDMO) that can be further explored in following link and note. This is a trigger-based solution which means that before the tables are initially migrated during the SUM Uptime Processing, the tool will create a set of triggers and logging tables for each of the tables so the newly business created data can also be migrated and be available after the conversion is completed. Note that this optimization only can happen when a migration of DB server is part of the maintenance event, either AnyDB to HANA or HANA to HANA leveraging homogeneous DMO.

The delta captured by the triggers on the logging tables is expected to be migrated before the downtime window begins to fully minimize the amount of data that needs to be moved during actual downtime. It is this transfer where the first focus should be, SUM uses its own delta record and replay technology (CRR) and as everything is business as usual, the logging (LT) and logging history (LTH) tables used by the CRR will continuously grow.

In cases like source database being IBM DB2/6, the LT and LTH tables are created as volatile which means no stats are kept for them so the access plan to this tables can and will degrade over time. In consequence the more changes are captured by CRR the slower the access to them will be, the impact can be minimal at first, but the access time will degrade over time primarily in the workload generation step (CRRCOLLECT), so the R3load processes will fetch less chunks per loop and row throughput will slow down.

This is because a VOLATILE table will have certain adjustments made to its statistics. If there are no statistics, DB2 will firstly fabricate some statistics and then modify them slightly. If there are existing statistics, the VOLATILE attribute will ensure the statistics meet certain conditions but will use the existing values as a basis for the adjustments that are made.

The adjusted statics are then used by the optimizer to formulate the access plan just like other tables. If it starts from a reasonably accurate estimation of the data in the table, the decisions the optimizer makes will be more accurate than if all attributes of the data are fabricated. In summary, without reasonable statistics to start with, DB2 will not know if the predicates provided in the query will return 1 row or 1 million rows.

Other SUM steps where the CRR is involved and will be benefitted by having updated stats on the bigger logging and logging history tables are (and not limited to):

  • RUN_RRC_REPLICATE_FINAL
  • EU_CLONE_MIG_OPTDMO_REPLAY_RUN
  • EU_CLONE_MIG_OPTDMO_COUNT

 

Downtime migration of tables

A separate group of tables are migrated in downtime and don’t have a delta, why a high-change-rate transactional volume would impact these tables that don’t have a delta to replicate is a good question. This phase gets impacted as a side effect as the splitting calculation happens as one of the initial steps of the recording window. Any additional extra time used during the recording window for the initial migration and the process of the recorded backlog will degrade the balance of the calculated splits increasing the risk of single long running buckets. Over splitting tables that have this symptom can help mitigate this unbalance in most cases.

In an extreme situation, whenever there is a downtime migrated table that has monotonic growth on the field used in the split condition where clause will have an unusual/unexpected behavior. This means that the range in this field which is evaluated when the splitting is done might differ considerably to the time the actual transfer is done, hence the final package will contain most of the data. For example, EDI40 and EDIDS tables grow on DOCNUM field and by default that’s the one considered for splitting.

In below example, EDI40 had in average 2 million rows in each split before bucket 100 taking less than 2h each, while split 100 migrated more than 59 million rows with a runtime of 19,6h.

Recommendation is to consider these tables for uptime migration if business won’t be impacted by CRR triggers during uptime. The change of the uptime/downtime table groups should be done after thorough analysis as for cases with heavily accessed/changed tables it might be required to fully migrate them in downtime to avoid impacting the system performance to the business during the SUM uptime activities.

 

FI and ML data conversion

What the SUM processes during the delta steps of the FI and ML comes from a set of protocol tables populated at RUN_RRC_FILLPROTTAB step. Little can be done to reduce the impact of the business to it as it grows almost linearly with keys added/changed during the recording window and that is not something that we can restrict the business on.

For the delta FI step (PARRUN_SMIG_SFIN), I saw that NZP (preparation step to prepare for the Delta data), MUJ (The actual migration of line items into ACDOCA from protocol tables from BSEG or FAGFLEXA, COSP, COSS, ANEX and ANEP) and R23 (the check of the migration of journal entries) were the bigger steps affected by the business load in the cases I worked on, and the number of periods to which the customer was writing the data was an additional important factor. Again, this cannot be restricted easily without additional impact to business, so the actual mitigation opportunities are limited.

But its important to note to accurately plan for the go live window without surprises.

On the ML data conversion side we see a similar situation on step PARRUN_SMIG_DT_MKPF1_INS and PARRUN_SMIG_DT_MKPF1_UPD, In this silent migration (sMIG) phases the newly created/changed material documents during the recording window with header table MKPF and item table MSEG are migrated to the S/4HANA table MATDOC, only mitigation that can be done is making sure enough BTC work process are configured, but it will only be long running if new keys are inserted frequently during the recording window.

On the other hand, step PARRUN_SMIG_DT_FILL_EXTRACT where the table MATDOC_EXTRACT is built from the MATDOC records is purely a downtime step. Runtime will come from the size of MATDOC table and won’t be impacted from the size of the delta generated/migrated, mitigations/optimizations on this phase should be done using the Parallelization Framework (PFW) as note 2351294 states for report NSDM_MIG_FILL_EXTRACT.

 

Other data conversions

The impact to other conversions steps like RUN_DO_CALL_XCLA_EXEC_DT should be mitigated with PFW configurations as well. While the delta data changes on KONV, VBFA will be kept up to date with triggers as they are part of the “Shadow Field Migration” process.

 

In summary

A high change rate transactional volume will impact both your uptime and downtime activities while performing a doC conversion but can be partially mitigated with analysis and planning. Uptime activities need to be outlined with the day-to-day activities to avoid any impact on the business, while having a bigger delta and longer recording window will impact the downtime as more data will be required to be processed and other side effects will affect on the downtime phases.

On the below example we ran multiple Load Verification (LV) cycles (executing uptime activities in production and finishing downtime steps in a cloned environment) and I have done my best to isolate all other variables that were changed from cycle to cycle, so using LV1 as baseline it can be seen how the number of recorded changes influenced the runtime.

Finally for the Go Live cycle I have included the mitigation activities in the calculation to showcase how much the runtimes can be reduced for some phases to help balance out the phases that are not so easily mitigated because it would involve restricting the business.

Each delta will grow differently based on business processes and needs, so your experience might vary.

If you are interested to know more, have some other query or excited to apply that knowledge in your conversion journey towards SAP S/4HANA via the above-mentioned Downtime Minimization methods, feel free to let your Technical Quality Manager know about it and copy us at SAP Premium Hub - CoE NA MO Team Inbox <sap_coe_na_mo@sap.com>.  Thank you!

entry.title: Help-me SDK DI API
entry.content:
Good afternoon.

I'm an SDK developer and I'm having some difficulties in development where I need to fetch some information from the DI API and I'm not getting it.
I need to return in the application I'm creating the database code that is in the SLD but I can't get this return via DIAPI.
Another problem is that I need to know how to get the HardwareKey and InstallNumber for my application to validate whether the user can use it or not.

Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

entry.title: Re: Do we have a way to migrate the Open Hubs from BWonHA...
entry.content: There is no option for migration of open hub, but you don't have to. If you have routine logic in open hub transformation, you will have to redo it. If not, there is nothing else that needs to be migrated.  Data Sharing in SAP DataSphere enables you to share datasets with other DataSphere tenants or external systems in a controlled and secure manner. You can set up data sharing agreements that define the scope, format, and frequency of data exports, replicating the Open Hub's capability of distributing data externally.

entry.title: Enhancing Security for SAP Customer Experience Solutions
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! 

Security is a top priority when implementing SAP Customer Experience solutions, which offer comprehensive tools for customer engagement and experience management. It is essential to adhere to SAP’s security guidelines and recommendations to ensure robust security. These guidelines cover critical areas such as user roles and permissions, data encryption, data protection compliance, system recommendations, GDPR compliance, access control, and secure provisioning processes.

We understand that navigating security complexities within these platforms can be challenging. To support your efforts, we’ve consolidated a set of centralized security-related assets, accessible through both the Security and SAP Customer Experience Solutions Value Maps. These resources will guide you in mitigating the risks of unauthorized access and data breaches, helping you maintain a secure environment for your customer experience operations.

Key Security Resources Now Available:

  • SAP CX AI Toolkit Security Guide: The SAP CX AI Toolkit enhances customer experiences with powerful AI capabilities. This guide provides valuable insights into securing AI-driven customer engagement and data processing.
  • SAP Sales Cloud Integration Security Guide: Learn about security best practices for integrating SAP Sales Cloud with other systems, ensuring data security and integrity across platforms.
  • SAP HANA Security Features: As a critical component of SAP Customer Experience solutions, SAP HANA's security features are essential for protecting customer data. The provided documentation offers comprehensive insights into securing this powerful database platform.
  • RACI Matrix for SAP Cloud for Customer: The RACI matrix helps define roles and responsibilities for managing SAP Cloud for Customer, including security-related tasks and decision-making processes.
  • SAP Upscale Commerce Security Guide: This guide outlines security best practices for SAP Upscale Commerce, offering recommendations to safeguard your e-commerce platforms.
  • Emarsys Customer Engagement Security Resources: As an integral part of SAP Customer Experience solutions, Emarsys security resources provide valuable insights into data protection and compliance.

By following the recommendations and best practices outlined in these guides, businesses can strengthen the security of their customer experience operations in the cloud.

Access and Support

Remember, to access the link above, you are required to complete a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, specifically designed for SAP Enterprise Support. You can find a detailed, step-by-step guide to registration here.

Any Questions?

Please, contact the SAP Value Map team! 

Sign up for the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps by requesting access to SAP Learning Hub or join the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps Learning Room if you already have access.

Stay Safe and Secure,

Your Security and Customer Experience Solutions Value Map Teams

entry.title: How to send notification (Success) after an object is created in BackOffice
entry.content: I have a custom button on wizard, I want to use NavigationService to notify user when an object is created successfully in BackOffice. However, this class is deprecated. It suggests to use com.hybris.cockpitng.util.notifications.Navigation, but the implementation DefaultNavigationService doesn't have any method. Does anyone have any suggestion how to do it?

entry.title: select query with for all entries not fetching the records
entry.content:
HI gurus, I am uploading excel file with bukrs gjahr augbl utr_no and utr_date into internal table gt_upload.
I have moved only 3 fields from gt_upload to lt_upload bukrs gjahr and augbl by looping gt_upload( though ideally not required to do so)

MY requirement is to fetch records from bseg based on bukrs gjahr augbl and shkzg as 'S'.

The lt_upload contains 126 records of which 125 records not exist with bukrs gjahr and augbl in the bseg itself as due to old data and quality system has not yet refreshed. I can see the last record exist in the bseg table.

I should get at least this record into my lt_bseg but, lt_bseg is showing 0 records.

I believe as per my understanding there should be 1 record which matches the criteria and I should get 1 record in lt_bseg which is not
happening.

What all are the possible reasons that it is not getting fetched from bseg when the criteria is satisfied ?

entry.title: Re: New SAP Community - Profile Signatures
entry.content:
@KjetilKilhavn hi !! My signature is working now, I just needed wait a bit after configured it.

Thank you !!

entry.title: Re: select query with for all entries not fetching the records
entry.content: Impossible to say without seeing your upload file, but remember to be mindful of conversion exits and whether or not you are searching with a value that would be displayed externally vs. the internal value you need to use for querying the database (specifically leading zero on AUGBL).

entry.title: Re: SAP EC Payroll (ECP) client copy and client refresh
entry.content:
Hi @rsnit ,

As per my understanding and this Note point 14, client copy copies only client specific data and client independent (cross-client) objects are not copied. As I want a fresh client hence I asked "Can we get QA-200 refreshed from P-100 (which is a blank system)". but it seems after copy from P-100 to QA-200 the client independent objects existing in QA-200 will remain and won't get deleted. Are you saying the client independent objects will be deleted in this client copy?

Regards

entry.title: Re: The impact of a high change rate business on downtime-optimized Conversion to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: Great write-up Bruno!

entry.title: Re: Changelog deletion with Process chain does not work
entry.content: Try to narrow the issue. is the change log/clean up process working when you do manually. If yes, try to create a new process chain and new variant with just 1 DSO for trail purpose and see if it is working.

entry.title: Re: select query with for all entries not fetching the records
entry.content: Thanks for response. If that was issue I should not have abled to fetch single record as shown in the first image where I can fetch with out using '*7000003*' at table level. It might be because bseg is cluster table, belnr might be mandatory field in where clause.............let me give a try with augbl using conversion exit

entry.title: Re: SAPI to ODP extractor conversion
entry.content:
Try this option.

1. Right click and select copy the existing transformation.

In the next screen, replace the source to new ODP source. Keep the target same and activate.

entry.title: Re: Hardware key for the sap system.
entry.content: I don't have saplicense anywhere in the pc, also where are you loging in ....?

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to query BPC model using SQL?
entry.content:
Member formula results are never stored. As a custom workaround, you can save the result using EPMSavedata. And then you can use the backend table where member formula are stored.

Also check sap note " 3019453 - Which table stores the member fomulas "

entry.title: Re: TM link between /scmtms/d_torrot and /scmtms/d_trqstg
entry.content:
Hi William,

/SCMTMS/TOR and /SCMTMS/TRQ are different Business objects.
Freight Units are stored in /SCMTMS/TOR BO. Right DB Table for Freight Unit Roots is /SCMTMS/D_TORROT, for Stops - /SCMTMS/D_TORSTP, Stages (STOP_SUCCESSOR Node) - /SCMTMS/D_TORSTS.

/SCMTMS/TRQ stores Transportation Requests. One of use-cases is storing OTRs (Order-based Transportation Requirement) and DTRs (Delivery-based Transportation Requirement) in standalone TM setup.

Best regards,
Uladzimir.

entry.title: Re: How to add parameters to GUI Transaction Fiori tile and access it in Custom Fiori App
entry.content: Bumping

entry.title: BW/4HANA - Unsupported Media Type
entry.content:
Hi Community, 

Was trying to create an Info Provider in our BW/4HANA Perspective on the SAP HANA Studio eclipse install but getting the error as show in the snapshot " Unsupported Media Type" .

Would someone be able to suggest how I could resolve this.  

Attaching the installation details and Installed software details in the snapshots as well.

entry.title: Re: Calculate Current Month, Last year same month, Previous Month, YTD, on SAC Report
entry.content: @Charlielin. Use the %GrandTotal() or GrandTotal() Mathematical Functions.

entry.title: Re: Calculate Current Month, Last year same month, Previo...
entry.content:
For Q2, use the %GrandTotal() or GrandTotal() Mathematical Functions.  See SAC Help.Sap.com
If this is all relative to Net Income as total, then divide each row by Net Income.

GrandTotal(Account)Returns the grand total of all the Account values in the result set. Filters are included in the calculation of the grand total.GrandTotal(Sales) returns the aggregated value of Sales.

 

%GrandTotal(Account)Returns the percentage of the grand total that each value represents.%GrandTotal(Sales) returns the percentage of the grand total that each value represents.

entry.title: [ByD] Notification rule for any changes in Supplier master data
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

 

I'm searching on creating rule to get email notification for any change in supplier master data.....but looks like this is not available in SAP, 

I was able to set up rule to get notified for bank data change or address change....wanted to make sure if any small changes can be notified.

 

Best regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: [ByD] Notification rule for any changes in Supplier m...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

You can create a Workflow Rule for the Business Object Supplier with the Rule Type as Notification. 

Select the Timing as 'On Every Save '. If you leave this field blank then the rule is applied every time the business object is saved.

Then consult the help documentation on Creating a Workflow Rule to define conditions and actions according to your specific situation.

Then active the new created rule, notification would be triggered to the maintained email address once supplier gets saved (changed).

 

Best regards,

Gavan

SAP Technical Support, Public Cloud ERP

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:

Here is the Validation 
1) Price with Currency
2) Overall Status Text

entry.title: Re: How to create AMDP in S/4 Hana Public Cloud ADT & Get...
entry.content:
Thank you for your question, @SiddharthaDas.

without any further context I could imagine a number or ways to take advantage of the abstraction and readily available services provided with core data services and their views without even the need to go down to the underlaying data model or entities.

Let’s dive into how you can consume standard CDS views in ABAP, especially with an emphasis on extensions in embedded Steampunk (SAP S/4HANA with Steampunk capabilities).

Consuming CDS Views in ABAP

When working with CDS views in ABAP, especially in the context of extending functionalities in embedded Steampunk, you will typically:

  1. Define and Extend CDS Views
  2. Read Data from CDS Views in ABAP Programs
  3. Use Views in Analytical Scenarios

1. Define and Extend CDS Views

You can consume existing standard CDS views or create your own. Let's start with an example where we have a standard CDS view and we want to extend it.

Standard CDS View

Assume you have a standard CDS view SAP_CDS_VIEW.

Extending the CDS View

If you need to extend this standard CDS view, you can use the EXTEND VIEW statement:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCDSVIEWEXT'@EndUserText.label: 'Extended CDS View'extend view SAP_CDS_VIEW with ZCDS_VIEW_EXTENSION {    new_field1,    new_field2}

 

2. Read Data from CDS Views in ABAP Programs

Once you have your CDS views (extended) as per need, you can read them in your ABAP code. In embedded Steampunk, the process is similar to classical ABAP but optimized for cloud.

Example ABAP Code to Read CDS View

Here’s how you can consume this CDS view in your ABAP program:

DATA: lt_data TYPE TABLE OF zcds_view_extension.
SELECT * FROM zcds_view_extension INTO TABLE lt_data.
LOOP AT lt_data INTO DATA(ls_data).  WRITE: / ls_data-field1, ls_data-field2, ls_data-field3.ENDLOOP.

3. Using Views in Analytical Scenarios

In analytical scenarios, you typically use CDS views for defining complex queries and projections. These views can be directly consumed in SAP Fiori apps or analytical tools.

Example: Using CDS View in Fiori

Suppose you have extended or created analytical CDS views tagged with annotations for analytical purposes:

 

@Analytics.query: true@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK@EndUserText.label: 'Analytical CDS View'define view ZANALYTICAL_CDS_VIEW as select from SAP_CDS_VIEW {    key field1,    field2,    field3,    @AnalyticsDetails.query.display: [        { position : 1, label: 'Measure 1' }    ]    measure1,    @AnalyticsDetails.query.display: [        { position : 2, label: 'Measure 2' }    ]    measure2}

Using ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse for Embedded Steampunk

When working in embedded Steampunk, you'd typically use ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse. Here's a brief on how to proceed:

  1. Create CDS Views: Use ADT to define CDS views.
  2. Extend Existing Views: Use the EXTEND functionality in ADT.
  3. Use @annotations: Utilize annotations like @Analytics.query to mark views for analytical use.
  4. Integrate in Your ABAP: Consume these CDS views in your ABAP classes or reports as shown previously.

Summary

  • Define/Extend CDS Views: Create or extend existing CDS views for your data models.
  • Read from ABAP: Use standard ABAP SELECT statements to query data from these CDS views.
  • Utilize Analytical Views: Mark your CDS views with appropriate annotations for analytical scenarios.

By following this approach, you can seamlessly transition your old ABAP skills into the new age of ABAP with CDS views, ensuring clean, maintainable, and efficient extensions in embedded Steampunk.

Feel free to ask if you need any more details or further clarification! Otherwise, please mark this question as solved. Thank you.

entry.title: Client Proxy - WS SOAP with different namespace for QAS & PRD
entry.content:
Hello to all!

I'm facing the following issue:

I'm consuming a WS SOAP using Client Proxy. This solution is in PRD System already.
The Web Service provider will change the namespace of the WS and will have a different one for QAS and PRD system.

What are the best aproach to solve this:

1. How can I change the namespace of an existing proxy?
Do I have to create it again from scratch?

2. Since the namespace in the Client Proxy is created in DEV System and cannot be change in QAS and PRD system, how is the best way to handle this?
Do I need to have two different Client Proxy and use one or another depending the system?

 

Thanks, Pablo

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear David 
I am Umesh 42years old, I am a highly motivated individual with over 20 years of experience in finance and costing. At this stage in my career, I am eager to transition into the IT field and have identified SAP as a promising avenue for professional growth.

I am particularly interested in your SAP learning program as a means to acquire the necessary skills to embark on this career change. I am confident that obtaining a SAP certification will significantly enhance my career prospects within the IT industry.

I am willing to join SAP learning initiative under change my career in midlife

Thanking you to consider me for the initiative.

UmeshOC

entry.title: Alternative storage location
entry.content:
Hi SAP support team,

We have a requirement where in case if stock is not available in one storage location then it should check another storage location and update in the sales order/Delivery

We request to Badi to fulfil the requirement. We know this requirement can be fulfill with a ATP with ABC confirmation but looking for alternative solution for development. 

regards,

Motiveminds team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Alternative storage location
entry.content:
Hi, 

For outbound deliveries, you can consider below BADIs:

LE_SHP_MODIFY_ITEM Modify Delivery ITEM

LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE Modify Standard and Custom Fields for Delivery Header and Item

 

Best Regard,

entry.title: Custom apps displayed as blank page in public cloud
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Hi Expert,


We followed note https://me.sap.com/notes/3274236/E to create a custom tile of My Inbox(F0862) to add new filters other than the default ones.
We added the expertMode parameter and assigned to the related catalog,

 

but after publishing, it only displays a blank page and is unusable...

I found that, for on-premise we can troubleshoot and clear the cache to solve the issue, but I am not sure how to handle it in public cloud...
Do you have any suggestions for solving this problem?

 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Find IQ Table Growth
entry.content:
Unable to get table information

Question and Answer:
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: IQ Performance & Tuning

Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Slow Server Performance

Q: Detail the IQ environment: exact IQ server version, OS version
A: Windows DIGITS, 16.1.030

Q: When did the issue last occur (YYYY,MM,DD))
A: DIGITS,08,15

Q: Is the issue reproducible on demand? If so please list the steps to reproduce the issue
A: No


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Find IQ Table Growth
entry.content:
it seems that you want to check IQ table growth from IQ system views.
There is no internal IQ system table/view having information to track data size of individual tables but you may check entire IQ data growth if you perform regular IQ full backups.
Please refer to KBA 2773774 below for more detail information.

--2773774 - How to find IQ data growth - SAP IQ
https://me.sap.com/notes/2773774

entry.title: Re: Parallel Processing of an report
entry.content: Try this: Implementing Parallel Processing (sap.com).

Thanks in advance.

Chempian Pontie

Using the web system landscape directorory mainly at

https://servername:40000/LicenseControlCenter/

or when you login it will take you to the control center go to service and select licence control

entry.title: Monitor Workforce person replicated from SAP MDI
entry.content:
We have replicated the workforce data from EC to MDI and further replicated from MDI to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Please provide guidance how to view and confirm in S/4HANA Cloud side for the replicated data from EC.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Monitor Workforce person replicated from SAP MDI
entry.content:
Dear customer,

The HR Administrator can monitor the replication status for each workforce person by following SAP Help Portal - Message Monitoring for Worker Integration.

In S/4HANA Cloud screen, it is also possible to check the successfully replicated workers in the app Manage Workforce or using the search model Worker Overview.

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Exporting contents
entry.content: Hi Anton, apologies for the confusion, I've amended the titile. I'm trying to export the object.

entry.title: Re: S4HC供应商寄售流程 Supplier Consignment (2LG)
entry.content:
Hi Qi

请问我在做从寄售库存转储到非限制库存时出现"无法计算覆盖范围"，不知道是哪里需要调整?
谢谢

entry.title: Is it possible to plan a product in all planning areas together?
entry.content:
There are several planning areas in the system, product planning is always against one specific product in one specific planning areas. Is it possible to plan one specific product in all planning areas? For example, all available stock of a specific product from all planning areas can be considered during MRP.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to plan a product in all planning area...
entry.content:
Product ID and Supply Planning ID are two basic parameters in product planning, it is impossible to run MRP against a specific product in all planning areas.

However, through setting procurement type under the Planning tab in material master data, and maintaining corresponding transport lane between internal sites, MRP in planning area A can create a stock transfer proposal in planning area B, thus you can transfer available stock from B to A to finally fulfill the demand in A.

Please refer to below help documents:

Material Planning:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2cc94b23722d10149641b01f63f17764.html

Source Determination in Planning:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/9b34fdf28661101498e8897131647296.html

entry.title: Re: Calculate Current Month, Last year same month, Previo...
entry.content: Apologized that I've given a bad example, the actual situation is that we have to use "Cross Calculation" to populate different measures on the row instead of just a single [account] dimension, that's why it's kinda complicated

entry.title: Re: Calculate Current Month, Last year same month, Previo...
entry.content: I've refreshed my screenshot to make it more clear

entry.title: Re: SAP workflow integration with external systems
entry.content:
Currently, there is no possible to rework the sales order for the external workflow.

Please check help document.

Thanks and Regards,

Celine

entry.title: How to check Document about Perpetual Inventory System and Periodic Inventory System.
entry.content:
現在、SAP S4 PERSON_NAME cloud publicでは、三分法ではなく、売上原価対立法が採用されていると思います。

売上原価対立法が採用されていることが明記されているドキュメントはありますでしょうか。

I think SAP S4 PERSON_NAME cloud public currently uses Perpetual Inventory System instead of the Periodic Inventory System.

If SAP has document in the community or KBA, mentioning that SAP supports Perpetual Inventory System (売上原価対立法) rather than Periodic Inventory System(三分法).


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check Document about Perpetual Inventory Syste...
entry.content:
Please Kindly use below link and search the related information. This is what I retrieved for you on Google.

SAP使用経験って、どの程度のことを言うの？ (fpacamp.com)

entry.title: Converting CS Notifications Category to PM Notification Category
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Customer Service (CS) will be replaced by S/4HANA Service (SAP Note 2269324).

For the foreseeable future we plan to use PM instead of S/4HANA Service. To this extend we would like to retain the current Notification Types as we have a large volume of history and custom enhancements based on the current Notification types. 

While it is possible to change existing CS Order Types to PM Order Types, it is not possible to do the same with CS Notifications. (Changing the Notification Category from "03" to "01")

We recognise it is not a simple change as there are many dependent tables using the Notification Category, but other customers must be facing the same issue. 

So the question is: Does other customers plan to switch existing CS Notification Types to PM Notification Types, and if so, how do you plan to achieve this?

Appreciate any help and guidance.

entry.title: Re: B1SLayer: A clean and easy way to consume SAP Business One Service Layer with .NET
entry.content:
Hello friends,
I have just discovered B1SLayer library and must start from shouting big "thank you" to Bruno for such a great contribution to SAP community.

I'd like to use the library to execute SQL queries. There is an example provided but it sounds like  it is missing a step. The text reads "Once your query is registered (don't forget the parameters), you can call it with B1SLayer..."; however, there is no code that shows how to register the query.

Could someone post a working example of a code that successfully executes SQL query, with all steps, including registration - thank you !

you were right, that configuration was missing.

Thank you!!

entry.title: SM：EXEC SERVICES並列処理数について(How to setup parallel job of SM:EXEC SERVICES)
entry.content:
担当者様

お世話になっております。

.....

「ジョブ名：SM：EXEC SERVICE」の並列処理数を減らすまたは直列処理とする方法についてご教授をお願いします。

以上、よろしくお願いいたします。

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Question:

Please advise how to reduce the number of parallel processes for Job SM: EXEC SERVICE.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SM：EXEC SERVICES並列処理数について(How to setup parallel job o...
entry.content:
ご担当者様

平素よりお世話になっております。

お問合せにつき、KBA 1992277 - Administering Parallelization Of Background Processing Of EarlyWatch Alert  をご確認ください。

以上、よろしくお願いいたします。

SAPサポート

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Solution:

Please refer to KBA1992277 - Administering Parallelization Of Background Processing Of EarlyWatch Alert

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Call for ALM
entry.content: Looking forward to this!

entry.title: Automatic approval for maintenance orders workflow is not working
entry.content:
Hi,

I created a reactive maintenance order, and according to the best practice for reactive maintenance (Reactive Maintenance (4HH_US)), we should be able to start a flexible workflow for approval. I sent the order, but it never reaches my inbox or gets approved. I can see the order in the Fiori application, and under the authorization status, I see that the automatic workflow has the order, but it’s been days, and it hasn’t been approved or rejected:

I thought the automatic approval workflow would approve the order instantly.

Does anyone know why this might be happening? Could it be due to a missing configuration? Is there a way to release this order from the current workflow to continue with the proces?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: SAP DBTech JDBC: [262]: invalid query name: JDBC_GET_TABLES
entry.content:
Hi, there

I got a JDBC Driver error when trying to get table metadata by JDBC DatabaseMetadata. It seems there's no proc or function named "JDBC_GET_TABLES":

com.sap.db.jdbc.exceptions.JDBCDriverException: SAP DBTech JDBC: [262]: invalid query name:  JDBC_GET_TABLES

As I dived deeper into its source code, more query names with the prefix "JDBC_GET_" emerged and they were all called by a strange statement "CALLQ", which I had never seen before. 

Since I do not have direct access to connect with the HANA DB, troubleshooting becomes difficult.  Same as connecting HANA DB, connecting the other DBs like MySQL, Oracle, and MSSQL also needs a jump server, but they work well. I enquired to the system admin and he told me there's no proc or function found in the HANA DB, this confused me more and wondering what "CALLQ" can be and how it works.

What caused this and how to solve it?

 

The query names:

The "CALLQ" Statement:

Java codes work well on Oracle, MySQL, and Microsoft SQL Server.

 

// Get connected DB Info
DatabaseMetaData dbMetaData = connection.getMetaData();

String dbType = dbMetaData.getDatabaseProductName();
String dbVersion = dbMetaData.getDatabaseProductVersion();
String dbUser = dbMetaData.getUserName();

// Get Table Metadata
String catalog=null, schemaPattern=null, tbl="T001", columnNamePattern=null;
String tblComment = null,tblCatalog = null,tblType = null,tblSchema = null;
ResultSet rs = dbMetaData.getTables(null, schemaPattern, tbl, new String[]{"TABLE", "VIEW", "SYSTEM TABLE", "GLOBAL TEMPORARY", "LOCAL TEMPORARY", "ALIAS", "SYNONYM"});
while (rs.next()) {
    tblCatalog = rs.getString("TABLE_CAT");
    tblSchema = rs.getString("TABLE_SCHEM");
    tblType = rs.getString("TABLE_TYPE");
    tblComment = rs.getString("REMARKS");
    System.out.println("Found [" + tblType + "] in Catalog [" + tblCatalog + "], Schema [" + tblSchema + "]: " + rs.getString("TABLE_NAME") + "(" + tblComment + ")");
}
rs.close();

// Get Column Metadata
List<TableColumnMetadata> metadataList = new ArrayList<>();
ResultSet columnsResultSet = dbMetaData.getColumns(catalog, schemaPattern, tbl, columnNamePattern);
while (columnsResultSet.next()) {
    TableColumnMetadata metadata = new TableColumnMetadata();
    String tblName = columnsResultSet.getString("TABLE_NAME")==null?tbl:columnsResultSet.getString("TABLE_NAME");
    metadata.setDbType(dbType);
    metadata.setDbVersion(dbVersion);
    metadata.setDbUser(dbUser);
    metadata.setDbCatalog(tblCatalog);
    metadata.setDbSchema(tblSchema);
    metadata.setTableType(tblType);
    metadata.setTableName(tblName);
    metadata.setTableComment(tblComment);
    metadata.setColumnPosition(columnsResultSet.getInt("ORDINAL_POSITION"));
    metadata.setColumnName(columnsResultSet.getString("COLUMN_NAME"));
    metadata.setColumnComment(columnsResultSet.getString("REMARKS"));
    metadata.setColumnType(columnsResultSet.getString("TYPE_NAME"));
    metadata.setColumnLength(columnsResultSet.getInt("COLUMN_SIZE"));
    metadata.setColumnPrecision(columnsResultSet.getInt("COLUMN_SIZE"));
    metadata.setColumnScale(columnsResultSet.getInt("DECIMAL_DIGITS"));
    metadataList.add(metadata);
}
columnsResultSet.close();

 

The Data Class:

 

import lombok.Data;

@Data
public class TableColumnMetadata {
    private String dbType;
    private String dbVersion;
    private String dbUser;
    private String dbCatalog;
    private String dbSchema;
    private String tableType;
    private String tableName;
    private String tableComment;
    private int columnPosition;
    private String columnName;
    private String columnComment;
    private String columnType;
    private int columnLength;
    private int columnPrecision;
    private int columnScale;
}

Srini

entry.title: Custom Field - SAP S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

The requirement is to create a custom field on the Manage Customer Master Data app inside the sales areas section and there needs to be predefined set of drop down values to be added to that custom field.

I know how to create a custom field in customer master, but not sure how to create one inside the section of sales areas tab under master data and also no clue on how to add drop down values to that.

If somebody could throw some insights on the above requirement would be helpful.

SAP Fiori SAP S/4HANA SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SD (Sales and Distribution) SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales 

Thanks!

entry.title: BAPI_DOCUMENT_CHANGE2: Document info record update object links - DOCUMENTDIRECTION
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are using BAPI_DOCUMENT_CREATE2 and BAPI_DOCUMENT_CHANGE2 for the management of supplier invoice documents. We need to pass DOCUMENTDIRECTION to the OBJECTLINKS table to enable navigation from the object link to the document in MIRO. Interestingly, when we manually add the link via gui, the document direction is automatically set to A, visible via BAPI_DOCUMENT_GETDETAIL2. 

However, despite our efforts to pass DOCUMENTDIRECTION to BAPI_DOCUMENT_CHANGE2- OBJECTLINKS along with OBJECTTYPE, OBJECTKEY and OBJECTKEY_LONG, our attempts have been unsuccessful. Although, the object link is saved, the document direction A is not retained. 

Has anyone in the community encountered this issue before? Your assistance would be greatly appreciated 🙂
